description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical woven bucket with a striking red and black striped pattern, giving it a visually bold appearance. The body is constructed from interlaced strips, likely dyed wicker or synthetic rattan, forming a sturdy yet lightweight container. A single, arched bail handle, possibly made of wood or wrapped metal, is attached to the rim with metal loops, allowing for easy carrying. The rim and base are reinforced with darker bands, providing structural integrity and a contrasting accent to the vibrant body. The interior appears hollow and open, suitable for carrying or storing various items. There are no visible lids, markings, or labels on the exterior, and the overall design suggests it is intended for practical use in urban or garden settings.
description: A tall, cylindrical woven bucket with red and black stripes, featuring a single arched bail handle and reinforced rim and base. The open design and sturdy construction make it suitable for carrying or storage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical shape, red and black woven pattern, and the full arch of the bail handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical alignment of the handle and the continuous striped pattern around the body.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the handle arch and woven pattern visible, but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the handle's vertical alignment and the uninterrupted woven design.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: dark woven reinforcement bands at rim and base
